decisions.title: Commit to Flink 2.0 migration timeline for production
decisions.context: Shadow dry-run infrastructure being set up (ENG-10077, ENG-10075, ENG-10076). Flink 2.0 job execution ready (ENG-9947). Need decision on cutover date to avoid dual-maintenance burden and unlock streaming performance improvements for Sift Edge.

arguments.for: Shadow testing infrastructure ready, Chainguard image hardening complete, enables real-time decision capture (North Star metric). Aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar through improved streaming foundation.
arguments.against: Gov deployment complexity (ENG-10070 encrypted storage), potential downstream impact on data-batcher (ENG-9998) and streaming consumers. May need staged rollout.

decisions.title: Canvas V2 launch readiness: Feature-complete or MVP?
decisions.context: Core Canvas work advancing: template components (ENG-10073), per-block editor (ENG-9957), output UI (DES-167), version history (DES-165, DES-166), comments (DES-164). Question: ship with full feature set or launch MVP and iterate?

arguments.for: MVP launch captures early customer decisions (North Star), enables 'Embed Deeper' learning loops, de-risks big-bang release. Version history can follow in 2-week sprint.
arguments.against: Version history/comments critical for team collaboration use case. Incomplete launch may signal unfinished product, hurt adoption. Canvas is 'Nail the Core' bet.

decisions.title: Resolve Explore V2 'Resume Live' bug before next customer demo
decisions.context: E2 Resume Live fails to return to live edge, stays at stale timestamp (ENG-9798). Explore V2 is flagship 'Nail the Core' product. Customer demos scheduled. Bug degrades real-time investigation workflow.
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause investigation incomplete. May relate to cached snapshot invalidation work (ENG-9789) or timestamp handling changes.

arguments.for: Critical path for customer trust. Live-edge investigation is core value prop. Blocking demos risks pipeline deals.
arguments.against: Workaround available (manual timestamp selection). Could defer if root cause requires architectural changes to CSRS caching.

decisions.title: Feature flag strategy: Fail-open or fail-closed by default?
decisions.context: Recent work removing feature flag checks (ENG-9951), reverting to default behavior on load failure (ENG-9995), FE prototypes underway (ENG-9122). Pattern inconsistency across codebase creates production risk.

arguments.for: Fail-open (default enabled) reduces outage blast radius, aligns with progressive delivery, enables faster iteration for 'Embed Deeper' experiments.
arguments.against: Fail-closed (default disabled) safer for half-baked features, prevents premature customer exposure, better for 'Build the Moat' stability. Need policy, not case-by-case.

decisions.title: Python Rules: Client SDK update (ENG-7055) blocks customer pilots?
decisions.context: Python client needs ABAC API updates to support new authorization model. ABAC policies evaluating archived resources (ENG-10039) suggests access control refinements in progress. Python Rules is 2026 'Build the Moat' product.

arguments.for: Customers expecting programmatic rule authoring. SDK gap blocks pilots, delays feedback loop. Critical for Python Rules adoption (moat-building).
arguments.against: Can provide REST API examples as interim. ABAC work may uncover breaking changes, prefer waiting for stable API surface.

keyResults.name: Explore V2: 90% of customers using live investigation weekly
Canvas: 50% of Explore users create 1+ dashboard
Ask SIFT: 40% query success rate (actionable answer)
Preset panel configs: 80% adoption for new investigations
ABAC policies: Zero unauthorized access incidents

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination week (32h meetings). Canvas design reviews (DES-164-167) and Flink migration planning consuming bandwidth. 1:1s with Explore, Canvas, Edge leads addressing execution blockers.
alerts: Thursday April 10: Zero focus blocks scheduled, 8 consecutive meetings
Canvas launch decision (Apr 10) conflicts with Board prep meeting
Explore V2 bug (ENG-9798) unscheduled but blocking Friday customer demo
